hero.title: 4 Days, The Taj Mahal.
hero.titleAccent: Agra, Mughal Splendour and a Love Story in Marble.
hero.intro: The Taj Mahal is the greatest monument to love ever built. This focused four-day trip puts it at the centre, with time for Agra Fort, Fatehpur Sikri and a taste of Delhi, so you experience the romance of Mughal India in full.

breadcrumb.label: 4 Days: Exploring the Romance of the Taj Mahal

hero.heroImageAlt: Taj Mahal Agra marble monument sunrise

overview.heading: 4 Days: Exploring the Romance of the Taj Mahal: The Full Picture
body: The Taj Mahal is considered the finest achievement of Indo-Islamic architecture, a rhythmic harmony of marble, light and symmetry built by Shah Jahan for his beloved wife. Seeing it in person, especially at sunrise, is something no photograph prepares you for.

This 4 day Taj Mahal tour package from Malaysia centres on Agra and its Mughal treasures, with a private vehicle and driver guide throughout. Beyond the Taj, you explore the mighty Agra Fort and the abandoned royal city of Fatehpur Sikri, with a taste of Delhi to round out the trip.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Taj Mahal at Sunrise
items.body: The marble shifts through soft pinks and golds as the sun rises behind the dome. Visiting early means the best light and fewer crowds, the most romantic way to experience this wonder.

items.title: Agra Fort
items.body: This vast red sandstone fortress was the seat of Mughal power and, later, the place where Shah Jahan spent his final years gazing at the Taj. Grand halls, marble palaces and river views.

items.title: Fatehpur Sikri
items.body: The perfectly preserved ghost city of Akbar, abandoned soon after it was built. Its red sandstone palaces, courtyards and the soaring Buland Darwaza gateway are extraordinary.

days.title: Arrive Delhi, Drive to Agra
days.description: Met at Delhi airport and driven to Agra, checking in for the night near the Taj.
suggestedActivities: Evening Mehtab Bagh sunset view of the Taj (optional)

days.title: Taj Mahal Sunrise & Agra Fort
days.description: Sunrise at the Taj Mahal, then Agra Fort and the marble workshops.

days.title: Fatehpur Sikri & Back to Delhi
days.description: Visit Fatehpur Sikri en route back to Delhi for some capital sightseeing.

days.title: Depart Delhi
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

tiers.hotel: 3-star hotels Agra and Delhi or similar

whyBook.heading: The Taj, Timed to Perfection
whyBook.body: The difference between a good Taj visit and a great one is the timing and the guide. We get you there at sunrise with someone who knows its history intimately. Dhesu has arranged Agra trips for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Taj Mahal Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
If the Taj Mahal is your dream, this focused four-day trip is the most direct way to experience it well. Agra is only a few hours from Delhi, and this itinerary gives you the Taj at sunrise plus the city's other Mughal wonders.

Going private means you reach the Taj before the crowds and enjoy it with a guide who brings its love story to life.

Want the full classic route? The 5 day Golden Triangle adds Jaipur to Delhi and Agra.

sections.heading: Best Time to See the Taj Mahal
sections.body:
October to March offers the most comfortable weather and beautiful light. Winter mornings can be misty, which occasionally softens the Taj at dawn, while clear days deliver the classic golden sunrise.

The Taj is closed on Fridays for prayers, so we plan your visit around that. We always aim for the sunrise slot for the best experience.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Delhi or Agra Airport (DEL / AGR). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Is the Taj Mahal closed on any day?
items.answer: Yes, the Taj Mahal is closed to tourists on Fridays for prayers. We plan your itinerary so your visit falls on an open day, ideally at sunrise for the best light and smaller crowds.

items.question: What is the best time to visit the Taj Mahal?
items.answer: October to March offers comfortable weather and lovely light. Sunrise is the ideal time year-round. Summers (April to June) are extremely hot, and the monsoon brings humidity and occasional rain.

items.question: Can I add Jaipur to make it the Golden Triangle?
items.answer: Yes. Adding Jaipur turns this into the classic Golden Triangle. Our 5 day Delhi Agra Jaipur package covers all three cities at a comfortable pace.
